latin: Boromir, cupiditate anuli captus, Frodonem oppugnare conatus est ut anulum ei eriperet. Frodo anulum induit et invisibilis fugit, nam intellexit se solum proficisci debere. interea Orci magni Societatem oppugnaverunt. Boromir, paenitentia motus, Merriadum et Pippinum fortiter defendit. multis sagittis vulneratus est, sed pugnare non desiit donec cecidit. Aragorn eum morientem invenit. Boromir culpam suam confessus est et in pace obiit, et Aragorn eum honoravit ut militem fortem.

questions.target: He was overcome by desire for the Ring
choices: He was overcome by desire for the Ring

questions.target: He put on the Ring and became invisible
choices: He fought Boromir with a sword
He put on the Ring and became invisible

questions.target: He was struck by many arrows while defending Merry and Pippin
choices: He was struck by many arrows while defending Merry and Pippin

questions.prompt: What did Boromir do before he died?
questions.target: He confessed his guilt to Aragorn
choices: He confessed his guilt to Aragorn

translation: Boromir, seized by desire for the ring, tried to attack Frodo to snatch the ring from him. Frodo put on the ring and fled invisibly, for he understood that he had to set out alone. Meanwhile, great Orcs attacked the Fellowship. Boromir, moved by repentance, bravely defended Merry and Pippin. He was wounded by many arrows, but did not stop fighting until he fell. Aragorn found him dying. Boromir confessed his guilt and died in peace, and Aragorn honored him as a brave soldier.
